remember: Students will be able to recall and describe the basic structure and functions of various ecosystems.
Students will be able to identify and explain the importance of different components of an ecosystem.
Students will be able to list and describe the different types of ecosystems and their characteristics.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of energy flow and nutrient cycling in ecosystems.
Students will be able to describe the role of decomposers in ecosystems and their importance.
Students will be able to explain the impact of human activities on ecosystems and their conservation.
apply: Students will be able to apply the knowledge of ecosystem processes to real-world scenarios and problems.
Students will be able to analyze the effects of human activities on ecosystems and suggest conservation strategies.
Students will be able to design and propose a sustainable ecosystem management plan.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze and evaluate the relationships between different components of an ecosystem.
Students will be able to identify and discuss the causes and consequences of ecosystem degradation and loss.
Students will be able to critically evaluate the effectiveness of different conservation strategies and policies.

ncert_objectives: Describe the structure and functions of different ecosystems.
Explain the concept of energy flow and nutrient cycling in ecosystems.
Discuss the importance of decomposers in ecosystems and their role in nutrient cycling.
board_exam_objectives: Describe the different types of ecosystems and their characteristics.
Explain the impact of human activities on ecosystems and their conservation.
Discuss the conservation strategies for maintaining ecosystem health and biodiversity.
